external_getSqrtRatioAtTick(int24).details: External wrapper for getSqrtRatioAtTick to allow vm.expectRevert

external_getTickAtSqrtRatio(uint160).details: External wrapper for getTickAtSqrtRatio to allow vm.expectRevert

testFuzz_approximation_sqrtToTickToSqrt(.details: Due to discrete ticks, we cannot expect exact equality. The recovered sqrtPrice      should be <= the original (since getTickAtSqrtRatio returns floor tick).

params.sqrtPriceX96: The fuzzed sqrt price to test

testFuzz_bijection_fullRange(uint256).details: Tests MIN_TICK, MAX_TICK-1, zero, and fuzzed positive/negative values.      Note: MAX_TICK is excluded because getSqrtRatioAtTick(MAX_TICK) returns MAX_SQRT_RATIO,      and getTickAtSqrtRatio requires input < MAX_SQRT_RATIO.

testFuzz_bijection_tickToSqrtToTick(int2.details: This is the primary bijection property: tick -> sqrtPrice -> tick is identity.      Note: MAX_TICK is excluded because getSqrtRatioAtTick(MAX_TICK) returns MAX_SQRT_RATIO,      and getTickAtSqrtRatio requires input < MAX_SQRT_RATIO (exclusive upper bound).

params.tick: The fuzzed tick value to test

testFuzz_consecutiveTicks_distinctSqrtPr.details: Ensures no "flat spots" in the tick -> sqrtPrice mapping

testFuzz_monotonicity_sqrtPriceToTick(ui.details: Higher sqrt prices should produce >= ticks (floor function behavior)

testFuzz_monotonicity_tickToSqrtPrice(in.details: Higher ticks should always produce higher sqrt prices

test_bijection_maxTick().details: Note: getTickAtSqrtRatio(MAX_SQRT_RATIO) reverts because it requires sqrtPrice < MAX_SQRT_RATIO.      This is by design - MAX_SQRT_RATIO is the exclusive upper bound.

test_knownValues().details: These values can be verified against Uniswap's reference implementation

test_revert_sqrtPriceTooHigh().details: TickMath.getTickAtSqrtRatio reverts with bytes("R") when sqrtPrice >= MAX_SQRT_RATIO

test_revert_sqrtPriceTooLow().details: TickMath.getTickAtSqrtRatio reverts with bytes("R") when sqrtPrice is out of range

test_revert_tickOutOfRange_tooHigh().details: TickMath.getSqrtRatioAtTick reverts with bytes("T") when tick is out of range

test_revert_tickOutOfRange_tooLow().details: TickMath.getSqrtRatioAtTick reverts with bytes("T") when tick is out of range

testFuzz_bijection_fullRange(uint256).notice: Tests bijection across the entire tick range using strategic sampling

testFuzz_consecutiveTicks_distinctSqrtPr.notice: Tests that consecutive ticks produce distinct sqrt prices

testFuzz_monotonicity_sqrtPriceToTick(ui.notice: Verifies that getTickAtSqrtRatio is monotonically non-decreasing

testFuzz_monotonicity_tickToSqrtPrice(in.notice: Verifies that getSqrtRatioAtTick is strictly monotonically increasing

test_approximation_maxSqrtRatioMinusOne(.notice: Tests behavior at MAX_SQRT_RATIO - 1 (highest valid input to getTickAtSqrtRatio)

test_bijection_tickZero().notice: Tests bijection at tick zero (1:1 price ratio)

test_knownValues().notice: Tests some known tick/sqrtPrice pairs for sanity

test_revert_sqrtPriceTooHigh().notice: Tests that sqrtPrice at or above MAX_SQRT_RATIO reverts with 'R'

test_revert_sqrtPriceTooLow().notice: Tests that sqrtPrice below MIN_SQRT_RATIO reverts with 'R'

test_revert_tickOutOfRange_tooHigh().notice: Tests that ticks outside valid range revert with 'T'

test_revert_tickOutOfRange_tooLow().notice: Tests that ticks outside valid range revert with 'T'
